original_text:
### Required Elements Within Sections
- Clinical claims require footnote markers (†) with footnote text clearly separated and noticeably smaller (§1.2).
- Patient quotes: attributed as `- [Name], LISRAYA patient`, italic or distinguished styling, paired with warm lifestyle imagery.
- Priovant logo, copyright line, ® legal line, job code [mm/yy], Indication, and ISI all live in the **locked header/footer components — never reproduce in sections.**

quotes: Clinical claims require footnote markers (†) with footnote text clearly separated and noticeably smaller (§1.2).

rules.intent: Ensure clinical claims carry required substantiating footnotes.
rules.rule_text: Clinical claims require footnote markers (footnote.marker.symbol = †) with footnote text clearly separated and rendered noticeably smaller (per §1.2).

quotes: italic or distinguished styling, paired with warm lifestyle imagery
Patient quotes: attributed as `- [Name], LISRAYA patient`, italic or distinguished styling, paired with warm lifestyle imagery.

rules.intent: Give patient testimonials consistent, humanized, attributable presentation.
rules.rule_text: Patient quotes must be attributed in the form `- [Name], LISRAYA patient`, set in italic or otherwise distinguished styling (text_style.patient_quote.italic), and paired with warm lifestyle imagery (image_treatment.photography.warm_lifestyle).

effect.subject: Priovant logo, copyright line, ® legal line, job code [mm/yy], Indication, and ISI

quotes: all live in the **locked header/footer components — never reproduce in sections.**
Priovant logo, copyright line, ® legal line, job code [mm/yy], Indication, and ISI all live in the **locked header/footer components — never reproduce in sections.**

rules.intent: Preserve single-source, regulated legal/ISI content in locked components.
rules.rule_text: The Priovant logo, copyright line, ® legal line, job code [mm/yy], Indication, and ISI all live exclusively in the locked header/footer components and must never be reproduced within content sections.
